title-group.article-title: Investigating the Use of Terrain Attributes in Characterizing Soil Geochemistry and Colour Properties

institution-wrap.institution: Brandon University, Masters in Environmental and Life Sciences
Brandon University, Department of Geography and Environment
Agriculture and Agri-Food Canada, Brandon Research and Development Centre

body.p: Everything is base on the 49 points only to compare to interpolated data see

caption.p: Table 1: Summary univariate statistics of selected geochemical and colour soil properties for each site (n = 49). Geochemical concentrations are reported in ppm, excecpt Ca and Fe(%).
Table 2: Summary univariate statistics of terrain attributes for each site (n = 49).
Table 3: Pearson’s correlation coefficients for select soil properties and terrain attributes (n=49).

tr.td: *** p < 0.001; ** p < 0.01; * p < 0.05; NS = non-significant at p = 0.05

caption.p: Table 4: Geostatistical parameters of the fitted semivariogram models of selected colour and geochemical properties within the agricultural and forested sites.

tr.td: 2 Strong spatial dependency (C/(C + Co) % >75); Moderate spatial dependency (C/(C + Co) % between 75 and 25); Low spatial dependency (C/(C + Co) % <25).

caption.p: Table 5: Model summary and performance statistics for the random forest regression using the training and test data sets.

Figure 1: Heat map of the Random Forest regression results showing the ranking of the importance of terrain attributes (based on % increase in Mean Squared Error) in explaining the spatial variabilty of selected colour and geochemical properties within the agricultural and forested sites. Top panel shows an average ranking for each site and across both sites.
